summary: Iteration 66 backend verification — Multi-Location + Last Date to Apply on Job Posting. All 12 spec cases PASS on pytest (12/12 = 100%). Multi-location list accepted alongside legacy `location`, case-insensitive dedupe works, `last_date_to_apply` validation (required + not past) enforced on pro POST and admin publish, admin drafts allowed without deadline. `GET /api/jobs?location=X` matches both new `locations[]` and legacy `location` fields (legacy jobs without `locations` still surface). Applications-Closed 400 gate fires on backdated deadline; `is_closed=true` reported in `GET /api/jobs/{id}`. Admin multi-location publish visible under `source=admin`. Backend-only run — no frontend testing this iteration as requested.

test_matrix.title: Pro creates job with locations=[BLR,HYD,CHN] + last_date_to_apply=today+7
Pro creates job with legacy location='Pune' only
GET /api/jobs?location=Hyderabad returns multi-loc + legacy-only jobs
test_matrix.notes: Both surface (case #1 multi-loc job admin-verified in test, legacy-only job with no `locations` array also surfaces with locations=['Hyderabad'] & is_closed=false)

test_matrix.title: Apply to job with backdated deadline yesterday
test_matrix.notes: GET /jobs/{id}.is_closed=true; POST /jobs/apply → 400 'Applications Closed. The last date to apply has passed.'

test_matrix.title: Apply to job with deadline tomorrow
Admin creates job with locations=[Mumbai,Pune] + last_date_to_apply=today+30
test_matrix.notes: 200; source=admin, locations reflected. Visible via GET /api/jobs?source=admin

test_matrix.title: Admin creates status='draft' with no last_date_to_apply

critical_code_review_comments: server.py `is_job_closed` uses server-local `date.today()` (UTC in container). If deadlines are intended to be interpreted in IST (India Standard Time), a job set to close on 2026-01-15 will flip to closed at 00:00 UTC on Jan 15 = 05:30 IST — 5.5 hours earlier than the user's calendar date. Consider parsing today() in Asia/Kolkata TZ for consistency with the audience.

jobs.py list_jobs: when both a role-based visibility `$or` (line 251) AND a location filter are present, the merge uses `{'$and': [{'$or': prev_or}, {'$or': loc_clauses}, q]}` which is correct — verified working via case #7. No action needed.

admin_jobs.py `_validate_common` still validates `application_deadline` future-only. That field is now legacy-parallel to `last_date_to_apply`. Consider deprecating one to avoid two separate deadline concepts on admin jobs.

JobPatchBody has NO `locations`/`last_date_to_apply` in server.py's model on line 735 — the field WAS added (line 741, 742) — verified present. However patching a job to `locations=[]` (empty list) would silently be ignored in jobs.py:376 (only truthy non-empty lists overwrite). Consider whether that's desired UX.

annotate_job_for_response is called on POST/GET/PATCH but NOT on /admin/jobs create response (admin_jobs.py:218 returns raw dict). The `locations` + `is_closed` fields are populated because they're written directly into the doc, but a defensive `annotate_job_for_response()` wrapper here would future-proof.

Pro-posted jobs default to verification_status='pending' — students won't see them until admin verifies. Test case #7 in the spec assumes the multi-location pro job surfaces to students; had to manually verify it in DB to make case #7 pass. This is EXISTING behavior, not a regression, but the spec wording is slightly ambiguous about that dependency.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test user (pro + student) created + one legacy 'Hyderabad' job inserted directly in DB for case #7. All cleaned up in autouse teardown fixture (users, their jobs, applications, and admin Iter66 test jobs).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 66 multi-location + deadline feature is green end-to-end on backend. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iter66_multi_location_deadline.py — reusable regression. For case #7 the pro-posted multi-location job needs verification_status='verified' bumped in DB to be visible to students; that reflects the admin-approval reality. Deadline logic is UTC-date based; if IST-date behavior is desired, expect a follow-up.
